message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My task: add error_threshold param to Image.quantize(). Likely touches same two files as your task - let's figure out how to split. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Adding sort_palette param to Image.quantize() for custom palette ordering after quantization. I need to touch Image.py (quantize method) and its test file. What's your feature/files?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features both need to touch quantize() in Image.py and its tests, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: we each edit the same two files but keep our changes line-level disjoint - I'll add my error_threshold param as a new keyword arg appended after your sort_palette param in the signature, add my own internal logic block, and add new test functions (e.g. test_error_threshold*) without touching your sort_palette code or tests. Please do the same for your sort_palette work. Agreed?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: We both genuinely need these 2 files (same quantize() method). To avoid clobbering: I'll add 'sort_palette=None' as the LAST param in the quantize() signature (after dither), and insert my sorting logic as a new block right before the final 'return im' (after the ImagePalette is built, ~line 1114-1116), leaving the self.im.quantize(...) call and everything above it untouched. Please add your error_threshold param BEFORE mine in the signature and put your logic near the self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans) call (~line 1109) or earlier, so our edits land in different lines. I'll add my tests as new test functions at the end of test_image_quantize.py - please do the same so our test additions don't collide. Proceeding once you ACCEPT.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed: I'll add error_threshold=None as a param BEFORE your sort_palette in the signature, put my logic near the self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans) call (~line 1109), and add new test functions (test_error_threshold*) at the end of the test file, distinct from your test additions. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed. Signature order: colors, method, kmeans, palette, dither, sort_palette (mine), error_threshold (yours, appended after). I'll only touch the block right before the final return im, and add new test functions at the end of the test file. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Implementation complete: added error_threshold=None as the last param in quantize() (right after dither), added a small _quantize_mse(self, quantized_im) helper method just above quantize(), and inserted logic near the self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans) call that iterates candidate color counts up to 'colors' and stops once MSE <= error_threshold (else falls back to 'colors'). Added 3 new test functions at the end of test_image_quantize.py (test_quantize_error_threshold_reduces_colors, test_quantize_error_threshold_uses_max_colors, test_quantize_error_threshold_met). All existing + new tests pass. I did not touch the final return im / ImagePalette block or anything after it, so it should be clear for your sort_palette insertion there.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: sort_palette implementation complete: added sort_palette=None as the last param in quantize() signature, with sorting logic (brightness/rgb key, using the existing remap_palette() method to reorder colors + remap pixel indices) inserted right before the final 'return im', after the ImagePalette block. Added 3 new tests at the end of test_image_quantize.py (test_quantize_sort_palette_rgb, test_quantize_sort_palette_brightness, test_quantize_sort_palette_invalid). All tests pass. FYI: my copy of the repo doesn't show your error_threshold edits landing - looks like we're actually in isolated environments, so no real clobbering risk, but wanted to flag in case that's unexpected on your end.
